ABOUT TECHNO BRAI NAFRICA
NEW 
FRONTIER FOR BPOS
Techno Brain is the leading IT Solutions, IT Training and BPO / ITES Services Company headquartered in Nairobi,Kenya with operations in Africa, USA, India, UK and UAE.
Techno Brain is committed to deliver high quality, cost effective and off-the-shelf solutions to Governments, NGOs
and Private organizations across the globe.
Our competencies and experience span the entire spectrum of ICT Solutions, IT Training and BPO / ITES services
Business & Technology Consulting
• Implementation Services System Integration
• Custom Software Development Product Development• IT Infrastructure Services and Managed IT Services• Business Process Outsourcing and IT Enabled Services• IT Training, Education and Capacity BuildingAFRICA
NEW FRONTIER FOR BPOS

The call centre and back-office related tasks and 
operations were being performed within their territories by the US and the UK until the late 1990s. India emerged as the BPO destination in 2000s; with Philippines taking the lead thereafter, by creating and providing employment to more than 750,000 people within its boundaries. One is to take note of the fact that the destination of BPO changes and evolves every decade, but why!
History has the answer to this - best price, quality, 
sustainability, availability of resources, among others.  Emerging business process outsourcing (BPO) destinations such as Eastern Europe, Latin America, South Africa, Czech Republic, Egypt and China are facing internal challenges to be a part of the outsourcing race. Amongst these, huge potential is seen in Africa and Europe. Many International BPO providers have set foot in Africa by way of acquisitions or direct investments; they have increased their share of local work while making the best use of the skilled labour pool available in the region for International  work.
60% of the African workforce is young, with a native English accent, associated more to the West.
East Africa has more than 20,000 jobs, 
catering to captive and non-captive BPOs, a good 10% of them are offshore jobs which are expected to grow by 50% in 2016.AFRICA
NEW FRONTIER FOR BPOS
As opined by a few of the International Consulting firms, Europe is the second-best client market for the $12.4 billion Indian BPO industry, helping generate 30% of total revenues. BPO service providers are entering the African market to cater to their International clients. Countries such as India have moved into mature business models, with high-end value chains, while Philippines is struggling to serve its customers due to extreme competition.
Africa is blessed with nature and rich talent. It has an 
abundance of young labour pool, the age ranging between 21 and 30.  60% of the African workforce is young, with a native English accent, associated more to the West. The number of fibre cables landing in various African countries have quadrupled in the past 3 years, with loads of TB data which can be transacted. The continent has the best and lowest telecom cost for International calls; for instance, one can call for as low as $0.029 cents from Kenya to the US, the UK, Canada, India, China, among other countries.
Africa. New Frontier for BPOs    |    3

AFRICA
NEW 
FRONTIER FOR BPOS
There are 200,000 jobs across domestic captives and BPO service providers in South Africa and close to 50,000 of them in East Africa. This provides investors with access to a significant pool of experienced talent. East Africa has more than 20,000 jobs, catering to captive and non-captive BPOs, a good 10% of them are offshore jobs which are expected to grow by 50% in 2016.  
Africa; with its rich talent pool and growing economy, can 
cater to various services such as call centre and back-office across a wide range of sectors. The key countries performing outsourcing services in Africa are South Africa, Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania and Rwanda. The Western African side has Ghana and Nigeria who concentrate more on domestic BPO, with French as their key language. The verticals currently handled are Healthcare, Telecom, ISP , BFSI, among others. 
Africa is the next best destination, after India and the 
Philippines, which can offer sizeable and good quality talent (English language skills) at a low-cost. The Latin American countries such as Argentina face challenges with provision of decent English language skills. Canada and Northern Ireland attract higher costs. Mexico and Czech Republic too are expensive relative to Africa, as per a few genuine reports.  
Africa, thus far, by experience is growing steadily on the 
BPO frontier. However, it needs tremendous support injected from Governments and relevant authorities to create more visibility and enhance an enabling environment. 
In summary, it will be interesting and exciting to watch 
Africa flourish. 
4   |    Africa. New Frontier for BPOs
